summary: Epic Dark Star, soaring St. Stephen and tight jams all night
blurb: The show rides a monumental Dark Star into a seamless St. Stephen/Not Fade Away flow before closing with a rapturous Turn On Your Lovelight. Jerry’s tone is other‑worldly, the rhythm section locks in, and the second set delivers standout Morning Dew and Easy Wind moments.
review: September 19, 1970 at the Fillmore East captures the Dead at a creative peak. The first half builds patiently through folk‑rock numbers before launching into a sixteen‑minute Dark Star that reviewers describe as “other‑worldly” and “angelic,” with Garcia’s tone soaring over Phil’s anchoring bass and Mickey’s cannon‑filled drumming. That cosmic trek segues flawlessly into Saint Stephen, then bursts into a high‑octane Not Fade Away/China Cat jam before the band lifts everyone with a joyous Turn On Your Lovelight. The second set shines with a heartfelt Morning Dew, an uplifting Easy Wind, and a triumphant Love Light finale that leaves the audience buzzing. Across all recordings the performance is consistently praised as “locked in” and “legendary,” making this Fillmore East night one of the era’s must‑hear shows.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four of the six analyses are explicitly positive, repeatedly lauding the Dark Star, St. Stephen and the tight rhythm section; only one mixed review notes poor audience fidelity but still praises the performance. The consensus is a strong, celebratory sentiment across multiple recordings.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard/matrix mix with crystal‑clear instrument separation and only minor noise; captures the full set in superb clarity, making it the definitive listening source.

key_highlights: A sixteen‑minute Dark Star that’s described as transcendent and angelic
Seamless segue from Dark Star → Saint Stephen → Not Fade Away leading into Turn On Your Lovelight
Powerful second‑set moments: Morning Dew, Easy Wind and an uplifting Love Light finale
Tight dual‑drum attack with Mickey’s “cannon” fills and Billy’s driving groove

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers an angelic, other‑worldly tone on Dark Star and leads the band with soaring solos throughout.
band_performance.Phil: Phil’s bass provides a solid, melodic foundation that carries the extended jams, especially during Dark Star.
band_performance.Bob: Weir’s rhythm work is tight and his lead interplay shines in the Not Fade Away/China Cat segment.
band_performance.Mickey: Mickey’s drumming is described as tight with explosive “cannon” fills that drive the high‑energy sections.
band_performance.Billy: Billy Kreutzmann locks in perfectly with Mickey, delivering a powerful dual‑drum groove that propels the set.
